revision.text:
{{ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®еҸӨеўі |еҗҚз§° = жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі |з”»еғҸ = |еҲҘеҗҚ = |жүҖеұһ = |жүҖеңЁең° = [[еұұеҸЈзңҢ]][[йҳІеәңеёӮ]]жЎ‘еұұ1дёҒзӣ® |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34|з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 2|з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 50.33 |зөҢеәҰеәҰ =131|зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 34|зөҢеәҰз§’ = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |еҪўзҠ¶ = дёҚжҳҺ |иҰҸжЁЎ = |еҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯ = пјҲжҺЁе®ҡпјү[[жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө]] |еҮәеңҹе“Ғ = йҠ…йҸЎгғ»йҮ‘йҠ…иЈҪйЈҫеұҘгғ»иӣҮиЎҢзҠ¶йү„еҷЁгҒ»гҒӢеүҜи‘¬е“ҒеӨҡж•°гғ»[[й ҲжҒөеҷЁ]]гғ»[[еңҹеё«еҷЁ]]гғ»[[еҹҙијӘ]] |йҷӘеЎҡ = |зҜүйҖ жҷӮжңҹ = [[6дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠ |иў«и‘¬иҖ… = |йҷөеў“ = йҒәзү©еҹӢзҙҚең°пјҡпјҲ[[е®®еҶ…еәҒ]]жІ»е®ҡпјү[[жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ]]ж®Ҝж–Ӯең° |еҸІи·Ў = гҒӘгҒ— |жңүеҪўж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ = |зү№иЁҳдәӢй … = еўідёҳгҒҜйқһзҸҫеӯҳ |ең°еӣі = Japan Yamaguchi |гӮўгӮӨгӮігғі = |гғ©гғҷгғ« = жЎ‘еұұ
еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = top }} '''жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі'''пјҲгҒҸгӮҸгҒ®гӮ„гҒҫгҒЁгҒҶгҒ®гҒҠгҒ“гҒөгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еұұеҸЈзңҢ]][[йҳІеәңеёӮ]]жЎ‘еұұгҒ«гҒӮгҒЈгҒҹ[[еҸӨеўі]]гҖӮзҸҫеңЁгҒ§гҒҜеўідёҳгҒҜеӨұгӮҸгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ е®ҹйҡӣгҒ®иў«и‘¬иҖ…гҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„гҒҢгҖҒеҮәеңҹйҒәзү©еҹӢзҙҚең°гҒҢ[[е®®еҶ…еәҒ]]гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠз¬¬31д»Ј[[з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ]][[зҡҮеӯҗ]]гҒ®[[жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ]]гҒ®ж®Ҝж–Ӯең°пјҲд»®еҹӢи‘¬ең°пјүгҒ«жІ»е®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ == жҰӮиҰҒ == еұұеҸЈзңҢеҚ—йғЁгҖҒйҳІеәңе№ійҮҺдёӯеӨ®йғЁгҒ®[[жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)|жЎ‘еұұ]]пјҲжЁҷй«ҳ107гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«пјүдёӯи…№гҒ®е°Ҹдёҳй ӮйғЁгҒ«зҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ[[жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј]]гҒ«зҷәиҰӢгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒгҒқгҒ®йҡӣгҒ«еўідёҳгҒҜз ҙеЈҠгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰеҮәеңҹйҒәзү©гҒҢжЎ‘еұұеұұй ӮгҒ«еҶҚеҹӢзҙҚгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ еўіеҪўгҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„пјҲдёҖиӘ¬гҒ«[[еҶҶеўі]]гҒЁгӮӮ[[еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі]]гҒЁгӮӮпјү{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲйҳІеәңеёӮеҸІпјү|2004}}гҖӮеўідёҳиЎЁйқўгҒ§гҒҜ[[еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ]]гғ»[[еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ]]гҒҢиӘҚгӮҒгӮүгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮдё»дҪ“йғЁгҒ®еҹӢи‘¬ж–ҪиЁӯгҒҜгҖҒеҸӨж–ҮжӣёгҒ®иЁҳйҢІгҒӢгӮү[[жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө]]гҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮзҹіе®ӨеҶ…гҒӢгӮүгҒҜиұҠеҜҢгҒӢгҒӨз§ҖйҖёгҒӘеүҜи‘¬е“ҒгҒҢеҮәеңҹгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒзү№гҒ«еҫҢжңҹеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҒӘгҒҢгӮүйҠ…йҸЎ2йқўгӮ’еүҜи‘¬гҒҷгӮӢзӮ№гҖҒ[[жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶]]гҒЁгҒ®дәӨжөҒгӮ’зӨәгҒҷйҮ‘йҠ…иЈҪйЈҫеұҘгғ»иӣҮиЎҢзҠ¶йү„еҷЁгҒӘгҒ©гӮ’еүҜи‘¬гҒҷгӮӢзӮ№гҒ§жіЁзӣ®гҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ гҒ“гҒ®жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўігҒҜгҖҒ[[еҸӨеўіжҷӮд»Ј]]еҫҢжңҹгҒ®[[6дё–зҙҖ]]еүҚеҚҠй ғгҒ®зҜүйҖ гҒЁжҺЁе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮйҳІеәңе№ійҮҺгҒ§гҒҜеҸӨеўіжҷӮд»ЈеҫҢжңҹгҒӢгӮүд»ЈиЎЁзҡ„еҸӨеўігҒҢзҜүйҖ гҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒӘгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒжң¬еҸӨеўігҒҜгҒқгҒ®еҡҶзҹўзҡ„гҒӘдҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гҒ«гҒӮгӮҠгҖҒз•ҝеҶ…[[гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©]]гғ»жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶гҒЁгҒ®дәӨжөҒгҒҠгӮҲгҒійҳІеәңе№ійҮҺгҒ®й–ӢзҷәгӮ’е·ЎгӮӢеҪ“жҷӮгҒ®ж”ҝжІ»жғ…еӢўгӮ’иҖғеҜҹгҒҷгӮӢгҒҶгҒҲгҒ§йҮҚиҰҒиҰ–гҒ•гӮҢгӮӢеҸӨеўігҒ§гҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮиў«и‘¬иҖ…гҒҜжҳҺгӮүгҒӢгҒ§гҒӘгҒҸгҖҒеүҚиҝ°гҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒ«зҸҫеңЁгҒҜеҮәеңҹйҒәзү©еҶҚеҹӢзҙҚең°гҒҢе®®еҶ…еәҒгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠ[[жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ]]пјҲ[[603е№ҙ]]жӯ»еҺ»пјүгҒ®ж®Ҝж–Ӯең°пјҲд»®еҹӢи‘¬ең°пјүгҒ«жІ»е®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҜжұҹжҲёжҷӮд»ЈгҒ®гҖҺжЎ‘еұұеҸӨеўіз§ҒиҖғгҖҸд»ҘжқҘгҒ®иӘ¬гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒзҸҫеңЁгҒ®еӯҰиЎ“зҡ„гҒ«гҒҜеҸӨеўігҒ®жҖ§ж јгҒҠгӮҲгҒізҜүйҖ е№ҙд»ЈгҒ®зӮ№гҒ§еҗҰе®ҡзҡ„гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲйҳІеәңеёӮеҸІпјү|2004}}гҖӮ === жқҘжӯҙ === * [[еӨ©жҳҺ]]5е№ҙпјҲ[[1785е№ҙ]]пјүгҖҒз¬¬7д»Ј[[й•·е·һи—©|й•·е·һи—©дё»]]гҒ®[[жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ]]гҒ«гӮҲгӮӢйҡ еұ…еҫҢгҒ®зҙҚж¶јдәӯе»әиЁӯдёӯгҒ«зҷәиҰӢгҖӮеүҜи‘¬е“ҒгҒҢеӨҡж•°еҮәеңҹгҒ—еұұй ӮгҒ«еҶҚеҹӢзҙҚ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲйҳІеәңеёӮеҸІпјү|2004}}гҖӮ * [[ж–Үж”ҝ]]5е№ҙпјҲ[[1822е№ҙ]]пјүгҖҒж–Һи—ӨиІһе®ңгҒҢйҒәзү©гӮ’еҶҚжӨңиЁјгҒ—гҖҺжЎ‘еұұеҸӨеўіз§ҒиҖғгҖҸгӮ’и‘—гҒ—гҒҰеӣіиӘ¬гҖӮжқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗгҒ®д»®еҹӢи‘¬ең°гҒЁгҒҷгӮӢиӘ¬гӮ’жҸҗзӨә{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲйҳІеәңеёӮеҸІпјү|2004}}гҖӮ * [[еӨ©дҝқ]]13е№ҙпјҲ[[1842е№ҙ]]пјүгҖҒй•·е·һи—©гҒ®гҖҺйҳІй•·йўЁеңҹжіЁйҖІжЎҲгҖҸз·ЁзәӮгҒ®йҡӣгҒ«гҖҒдёүз”°е°»е®°еҲӨгҒҢйҒәзү©гӮ’еҶҚжӨңиЁјгҒ—зөөеӣідҪңжҲҗ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ * [[1902е№ҙ]]пјҲ[[жҳҺжІ»]]35е№ҙпјүгҖҒе®®еҶ…зңҒпјҲзҸҫгғ»[[е®®еҶ…еәҒ]]пјүгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠйҒәзү©еҶҚеҹӢзҙҚең°гҒҢжқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗгҒ®ж®Ҝж–Ӯең°гҒ«жІ»е®ҡ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ == еҮәеңҹе“Ғ == гҖҺйҳІй•·йўЁеңҹжіЁйҖІжЎҲгҖҸгҒ®иЁҳијүзӯүгҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҸеҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒҜж¬ЎгҒ®йҖҡгӮҠ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ * йҠ…йҸЎ 2 * зҹіиЈҪжЁЎйҖ е“Ғ 1 * йү„еҲҖ 3 * йү„зҹӣ 5 * йү„йҸғ иӢҘе№І * з”ІеҶ‘ * иӣҮиЎҢзҠ¶йү„еҷЁ 1 * йҮ‘йҠ…иЈҪйЈҫеұҘ * йҠҖйӯҡзӯүд»ҳеұһиЈ…йЈҫеҷЁзү© * йҚҚйҮ‘йҲҙ * зҺүйЎһ - з®ЎзҺү13гҖҒе°ҸзҺү60д»ҘдёҠгҖӮ * йҰ¬е…· - йҸЎжқҝгҖҒиҫ»йҮ‘е…·гҖҒиҪЎгҖҒйһҚйҮ‘е…·гҖҒйҗҷгҖҒжқҸи‘үгҖӮ * [[й ҲжҒөеҷЁ]] - еәғеҸЈеЈәгҖҒеҷЁеҸ°гҖҒрӨӯҜгҒӘгҒ©гҖӮ * [[еңҹеё«еҷЁ]] - й«ҳеқҸгҖӮ * [[еҹҙијӘ]] - еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘгҖҒеҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘгҖӮ д»ҘдёҠгҒ®гҒ»гҒӢгҒ«гӮӮиЁҳијүгҒ«жјҸгӮҢгҒҹеүҜи‘¬е“ҒгҒҢзӣёеҪ“ж•°гҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒЁиҰӢгӮүгӮҢгҖҒгҒқгҒ®иұҠеҜҢгҒ•гғ»з§ҖйҖёгҒ•гҒҢжіЁзӣ®гҒ•гӮҢгҖҒзү№гҒ«[[жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶]]зі»ж–ҮеҢ–гҒ®иүІжҝғгҒ•гҒҢжҢҮж‘ҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮеүҜи‘¬е“ҒгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎгҒ§гҒҜзү№гҒ«гҖҒйҮ‘йҠ…иЈҪйЈҫеұҘгҒ®еҮәеңҹгҒҢд»–гҒ«[[жұҹз”°иҲ№еұұеҸӨеўі]]пјҲ[[зҶҠжң¬зңҢ]]пјүгғ»[[зЁІиҚ·еұұеҸӨеўі (й«ҳеі¶еёӮ)|зЁІиҚ·еұұеҸӨеўі]]пјҲ[[ж»ӢиіҖзңҢ]]пјүгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®гҒҝгҖҒиӣҮиЎҢзҠ¶йү„еҷЁгҒҢд»–гҒ«[[йЈӣйіҘеҜә]]еЎ”еҝғзӨҺгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®гҒҝгҒЁж•°е°‘гҒӘгҒ„еҮәеңҹдҫӢгҒ§гҒӮгӮҠгҖҒеҠ гҒҲгҒҰйҮ‘йҠ…иЈҪйЈҫеұҘгҒЁиӣҮиЎҢзҠ¶йү„еҷЁгҒ®е…ұдјҙгҒҜжң¬еҸӨеўігҒ®гҒҝгҒ«гҒӘгӮӢ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ еӨ©жҳҺ5е№ҙпјҲ1785е№ҙпјүгҒ®еҸӨеўізҷәиҰӢеҫҢгҖҒеҮәеңҹйҒәзү©гҒҜзҹіеҢЈпјҲзҹігҒ®з®ұпјүгҒ«е…ҘгӮҢгҒҰжЎ‘еұұеұұй ӮгҒ«еҶҚеҹӢзҙҚгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹ{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮгҒқгҒ®еҫҢгӮӮж–Үж”ҝ5е№ҙпјҲ1822е№ҙпјүгғ»еӨ©дҝқ13е№ҙпјҲ1842е№ҙпјүгҒ«йҒәзү©гҒ®еҶҚжӨңиЁјгҒҢгҒӘгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒҢгҖҒ1902е№ҙпјҲжҳҺжІ»35е№ҙпјүгҒ«е®®еҶ…зңҒгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠйҒәзү©еҶҚеҹӢзҙҚең°гҒҢжқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗж®Ҝж–Ӯең°гҒ«жІ»е®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒӢгӮүзҸҫеңЁгҒ«иҮігӮӢгҒҫгҒ§гҒҜе®®еҶ…еәҒгҒ®з®ЎзҗҶдёӢгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒиӘҝжҹ»гҒҜе®ҹж–ҪгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒӘгҒ„{{Sfn|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўіпјҲеұұеҸЈзңҢеҸІпјү|2000}}гҖӮ {{Infobox Ancient Tomb |Name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |Image = |Alternate Name = |Affiliation = |Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө}} |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Burial mound no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of Emperor {{ill|YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs of Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]]

{{Infobox Ancient Tomb |Name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |Image = |Alternate Name = |Affiliation = |Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө}} |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Burial mound no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of Emperor {{ill|YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. (temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of Emperor {{ill|YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of Emperor {{ill|YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and figurative {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |Image = |Alternate Name = |Affiliation = |Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө}} |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Burial mound no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |Image = |Alternate Name = |Affiliation = |Location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө}} |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Burial mound no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]]

TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]]

Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |Alternate name = |Affiliation = |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө}} |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Burial mound no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:dd]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |Alternate name = |Affiliation = |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө}} |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Burial mound no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |Alternate name = |Affiliation = |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө}} |Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Burial mound no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The burial mound no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the burial mound was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The burial mound's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|й•·е·һи—©}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, saddle fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji ware|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]]
