Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |Alternate name = |Affiliation = |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 2|Latitude Seconds = 50.33 |Longitude Degrees = 131|Longitude Minutes = 34|Longitude Seconds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |Shape = Unknown |Dimensions = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |Cultural Heritage Status = None |Notes = Kofun no longer exists |Map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |Label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label Position = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original shape is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |Affiliation = |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 2|coordinatesonds = 50.33 |coordinatesrees = 131|coordinatesutes = 34|coordinatesonds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |type = Unknown |area = |Burial Facility = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-typed iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |other_designation Status = None |Notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |map_dot_label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-typed tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-typed iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-typed iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-typed iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |coordinatesrees = 34|coordinatesutes = 2|coordinatesonds = 50.33 |coordinatesrees = 131|coordinatesutes = 34|coordinatesonds = 4.58 |ISO = JP-35 |type = Unknown |area = |burial = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied artifacts: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |other_designation Status = None |Notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Yamaguchi |Icon = |map_dot_label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the artifacts were reburied is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and artifacts were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == Artifacts == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous artifacts were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the artifacts and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The artifacts were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designated the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following artifacts were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These artifacts, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the artifacts were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}{{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, Kuwano 1-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 2|coordinates = 50.33 |coordinates = 131|coordinates = 34|coordinates = 4.58 |omitted = JP-35 |type = Unknown |area = |burial = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6дё–зҙҖ}} |Burial Figure = |Imperial Tomb = Site of reburied burial goods: Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} |other_designation Status = None |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Yamaguchi |type = |map_dot_label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} designation1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|еҸӨеўі|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|HЕҚfu|ja|йҳІеәңеёӮ}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|еұұеҸЈзңҢ}}, Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is other_designation1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}} as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}}, the son of {{ill|Emperor YЕҚmei|ja|з”ЁжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©}} and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|ж–Үж”ҝ}} 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ({{ill|TenpЕҚ|ja|еӨ©дҝқ}} 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ}} other_designation1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|й ҲжҒөеҷЁ|1=Sue pottery}} (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|еңҹеё«еҷЁ|1=Haji pottery}} (tall cups) * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|еҹҙијӘ}} (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs other_designation in 1902, it has been under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|е®®еҶ…еәҒ}}, preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ}} * {{ill|Dainichi Kofun|ja|еӨ§ж—ҘеҸӨеўі}} * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)}} - Discovered during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit.
