(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:еұұеҸЈзңҢгҒ®еҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |part_of = |location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]][[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], Kuwano 1-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 2|coordinates = 50.33 |coordinates = 131|coordinates = 34|coordinates = 4.58 |omitted = JP-35 |type = Unknown |area = |burial = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Satellite Tombs = |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = Site of reburied burial goods: Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume|qq=}} |designation1 = None |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Yamaguchi |type = |map_dot_label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |part_of = |location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]][[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], Kuwano 1-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 2|coordinates = 50.33 |coordinates = 131|coordinates = 34|coordinates = 4.58 |omitted = JP-35 |type = Unknown |area = |burial = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Satellite Tombs = |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = Site of reburied burial goods: Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume|qq=}} |designation1 = None |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Yamaguchi |type = |map_dot_label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[:en:1785|1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[:en:1822|1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[:en:1785|1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[:en:1822|1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[:en:1842|1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[:en:1902|1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[:en:1785|1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[:en:1822|1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[:en:1842|1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[:en:1902|1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of HЕҚfu]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[:en:1785|1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[:en:1822|1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[:en:1842|1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[:en:1902|1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[:en:1822|1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[:en:1842|1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[:en:1902|1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |part_of = |location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]][[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], Kuwano 1-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 2|coordinates = 50.33 |coordinates = 131|coordinates = 34|coordinates = 4.58 |omitted = JP-35 |type = Unknown |area = |burial = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Satellite Tombs = |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = Site of reburied burial goods: Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume|qq=}} |designation1 = None |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Yamaguchi |type = |map_dot_label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[:en:1785|1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[:en:1822|1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[:en:1842|1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[:en:1902|1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |part_of = |location = [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]][[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], Kuwano 1-chome |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 2|coordinates = 50.33 |coordinates = 131|coordinates = 34|coordinates = 4.58 |omitted = JP-35 |type = Unknown |area = |burial = Presumed {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=жЁӘз©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|4=zh|5=ж©«з©ҙејҸзҹіе®Ө|6=ko|7=көҙмӢқ лҸҢл°©л¬ҙлҚӨ|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, snake-shaped iron tools, numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] |Satellite Tombs = |built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = Site of reburied burial goods: Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of Prince {{ill|Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume|qq=}} |designation1 = None |notes = Kofun no longer exists |map = Japan Yamaguchi |type = |map_dot_label = Kuwano
TЕҚno-o Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifact reburial site at the summit of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ (йҳІеәңеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}
(temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}})}}]] '''Kuwano-yama TЕҚno-o Kofun''' (жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі) was an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:HЕҚfu|HЕҚfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Kofun no longer exists. While the identity of the interred remains unclear, the site where the burial goods were reburied is Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the temporary burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, the son of [[:en:Emperor YЕҚmei|Emperor YЕҚmei]]. == Overview == The tomb was built on a small knoll midway up the slope of {{ill|Mount Kuwano|ja|жЎ‘еұұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kuwano|qq=}}, located in the central HЕҚfu Plain of southern Yamaguchi Prefecture. Discovered during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kofun was destroyed at the time, and burial goods were reburied at the mountain's summit. The Kofun's original type is unknown; some theories suggest a {{ill|circular mound|ja|еҶҶеўі|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular mound|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. Excavation records indicate the presence of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|еҶҶзӯ’еҹҙијӘ|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|еҪўиұЎеҹҙијӘ|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber based on historical documents. The tomb yielded a rich collection of grave goods, including two bronze mirrors, gilt bronze ornamental shoes, and snake-shaped iron tools, pointing to connections with the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|жңқй®®еҚҠеі¶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The tomb is dated to the early 6th century during the late Kofun period. This kofun is considered pivotal in examining the development of the HЕҚfu Plain and its ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] and the Korean Peninsula. Although Prince Kume is officially associated with this site, scholarly analysis refutes the connection due to discrepancies in the construction period. Another hypothesis suggests the [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] in HЕҚfu as the actual temporary burial site. == History == * [[:en:1785|1785]] ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5): The tomb was discovered during the construction of a summer retreat for {{ill|MЕҚri Shigenari|ja|жҜӣеҲ©йҮҚе°ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚri Shigenari|qq=}}, the 7th daimyo of the {{ill|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|ja|ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚshЕ« Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Numerous burial goods were unearthed and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. * [[:en:1822|1822]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 5): SaitЕҚ Sadayoshi examined the burial goods and published the ''Kuwano Kofun Private Study,'' proposing the tomb as Prince Kume's temporary burial site. * [[:en:1842|1842]] ([[:en:TenpЕҚ|TenpЕҚ]] 13): The burial goods were reevaluated during the compilation of the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' and an illustration of the tomb was created. * [[:en:1902|1902]]: The [[:en:Ministry of the Imperial Household|Imperial Household Ministry]] Historic Site1 the reburial site as Prince KumeвҖҷs temporary burial site. == burial goods == Based on records such as the ''BЕҚchЕҚ Fudoki,'' the following burial goods were excavated: * Two bronze mirrors * One stone imitation object * Three iron swords * Five iron halberds * Several iron arrowheads * Armor * One snake-shaped iron tool * Gilt bronze ornamental shoes * Silverfish ornaments * Gilded bells * Jade beads (13 tubular beads, over 60 small beads) * Horse gear (mirror plates, corner fittings, bridles, sa11le fittings, stirrups, pendants) * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (wide-mouthed jars, pedestaled bowls, spouted vessels) * [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (tall cups) * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (cylindrical and figurative) These burial goods, which highlight significant cultural exchange with the Korean Peninsula, were notable for their richness and exceptional quality. Items like the gilt bronze ornamental shoes and snake-shaped iron tools have few parallels and are considered unique to this tomb. After its discovery in 1785, the burial goods were sealed in a stone casket and reburied at the summit of Mount Kuwano. Since the siteвҖҷs Historic Site in 1902, it has been under the management of the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], preventing further investigations. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor=|author=жЎ‘еҺҹйҡҶеҚҡ|year=1989|chapter=еЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|title=Infobox ancient site Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|жқҘзӣ®зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * [[:en:Dainichi Kofun|Dainichi Kofun]] * {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|ja|еЎҡз©ҙеҸӨеўі (зҫҪжӣійҮҺеёӮ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}} - Designated tomb of Prince Kume. * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі|version=99995280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kuwasan TЕҚno-o Kofun]] [[ja:жЎ‘еұұеЎ”гғҺе°ҫеҸӨеўі]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamaguchi prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:йҳІеәңгҒ® Municipal History]]
